close_reason: Type ascription syntax is not stable Rust; as cast is the standard pattern
description:
src/lib.rs:170, 177 use 'Ok(mi as Arc<dyn TokenCredential>)' to coerce Arc<ManagedIdentityCredential> to Arc<dyn TokenCredential>. The unsized coercion is automatic in many contexts, so the 'as' is unnecessary — but the function return type is the trait object so the coercion fires here too.

Idiomatic: drop the 'as' cast, since the function signature already names the target type.

  CredentialMode::ManagedIdentity => {
      ManagedIdentityCredential::new(None)
          .map(|c| c as Arc<dyn TokenCredential>) // remove 'as'
          .map_err(...)
  }

Or, since the function returns Arc<dyn TokenCredential>, just:
  Ok(ManagedIdentityCredential::new(None).map_err(...)?)

The compiler will coerce. Filed as opinion because the current code is unambiguous and clippy does not flag it.
title: Explicit 'as Arc<dyn TokenCredential>' could be a type ascription on the binding
